title: lonicera korolkowii var. longibracteolata
intro: Lonicera korolkowii var. longibracteolata, also known as the Korolkow's honeysuckle or long-bracted honeysuckle, is a plant species within the honeysuckle family, characterized by its fragrant white flowers and long-bracted leaves. This plant is used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, antiviral, and anti-inflammatory properties. The medicinal properties of Lonicera korolkowii var. longibracteolata are believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving cough and sore throats, and treating digestive issues. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
